title: Orbán Exits Parliament After 36 Years, Names Gulyás as Opposition Leader
detail: Viktor Orbán announced on April 25, 2026 that he would not take up his parliamentary seat following his landslide election defeat, ending 36 years of near-continuous parliamentary service since 1990. Orbán said he would remain as Fidesz party leader and rebuild Hungary's 'national side' from outside the legislature. Gergely Gulyás — the former Minister of the Prime Minister's Cabinet — will lead the Fidesz parliamentary faction as opposition leader. The move was described as seismic: Orbán had personally dominated Hungarian parliamentary life for three decades, and stepping out while retaining party leadership reflects a deliberate positioning for eventual return.

media.caption: Orbán ends 36-year parliamentary career, handing faction leadership to Gulyás

title: Magyar Warns Orbán-Linked Oligarchs Fleeing Hungary with Billions
detail: Incoming Prime Minister Péter Magyar issued an urgent warning on April 25 that businesspeople tied to Viktor Orbán's outgoing government were moving billions of forints abroad — to destinations including the UAE, Uruguay, and the United States. Magyar specifically named billionaire Lőrinc Mészáros — Orbán's childhood friend and Hungary's richest man — as among those moving their families to Dubai, with children reportedly withdrawn from school and private security arranged for departure. Magyar called on law enforcement and courts to freeze assets and prevent the departure of key figures, framing the flight as an attempt to escape accountability under his incoming anti-corruption administration. Hungary's mining authority separately launched a preliminary investigation into a company linked to the Orbán family.

media.caption: Magyar warns investors against Orbán-linked assets as oligarchs reportedly flee Hungary
